steps.title: Extract points from the table
steps.content: The table gives three points on the line: $(-6,\, n+184)$, $(-3,\, n+92)$, and $(0,\, n)$. Since the relationship is linear, any two points yield the same slope.

steps.title: Compute the slope
steps.content:
Using points $(-6,\, n+184)$ and $(-3,\, n+92)$:
$$m = \frac{(n+92)-(n+184)}{-3-(-6)} = \frac{-92}{3} = -\frac{92}{3}$$
The $n$ cancels, leaving a purely numerical result.

steps.title: Verify with the third point
steps.content:
Using points $(-3,\, n+92)$ and $(0,\, n)$:
$$m = \frac{n-(n+92)}{0-(-3)} = \frac{-92}{3}$$
Consistent. Now test every choice against $m = -\dfrac{92}{3}$:
  • Choice A — $-\frac{92}{3}$: Equals our computed slope exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — $-\frac{3}{92}$: This is the reciprocal of A (not the slope). ✗
  • Choice C — $\frac{n+92}{-3}$: Still contains $n$, but the slope is a fixed number; this is not the slope formula applied correctly. ✗
  • Choice D — $\frac{2n-92}{3}$: Contains $n$; would only equal $-\frac{92}{3}$ if $n=0$, which is not guaranteed. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
